items.rationale: Repository-local ignore patterns and generated artifacts do not affect runtime parity behavior.
Local operator guidance; not part of Hermes runtime behavior.
Contributor process guidance and release policy; no runtime behavior.

Hermes Agent Ultra intentionally ships a Rust multi-stage container with tini/gosu and /data state instead of upstream's Python uv+s6-overlay image; Rust-native container contracts cover the retained runtime behavior.
Licensing and attribution metadata; no runtime effect.

README.md intentionally describes Ultra's Rust-first runtime, release installer, and parity workflow rather than upstream's Python product positioning; install/readme contracts cover the actionable setup guidance.
Compose defaults intentionally target the Ultra Rust image, /data volume, and hermes-agent-ultra naming while preserving localhost dashboard and UID/GID runtime controls.
The entrypoint intentionally implements the Rust image's direct tini/gosu startup path instead of upstream's s6 stage2 hook; entrypoint contracts cover home setup, UID/GID remap, and privilege drop.
Nix packaging intentionally builds the Rust workspace with the Hermes Ultra release version rather than upstream's Python flake-parts packaging stack.
Homebrew packaging uses Hermes Ultra's checksummed Rust release artifacts instead of upstream's Python virtualenv formula.
Distribution packaging path impacts install parity for Homebrew users.
Optional skill catalog content is curated locally; Hermes Ultra tracks runtime parity through native skill-loader contracts instead of vendoring every upstream Python skill implementation.
Upstream optional-skills blockchain tree is Python-skill-store content; Hermes Ultra keeps Rust-first runtime parity and supports external skill registries via native loader paths.
Upstream optional-skills MLOps content is curated catalog material; Hermes Ultra tracks it through skill catalog governance while keeping runtime execution Rust-first.

Installer intentionally downloads or builds Hermes Agent Ultra Rust release binaries instead of cloning upstream's Python/uv environment; installer contracts cover release assets, symlinks, PATH guard, Termux pathing, and post-install probes.
Top-level test differences can encode behavior contracts and must be mapped to Rust-native or local contract-test coverage before being cleared.
Gateway test deltas exercise platform adapter behavior and must be reviewed as runtime parity coverage.
Documentation-site content differs by project positioning and release policy; runtime behavior is unaffected.
Tool tests cover command, policy, and backend semantics that affect runtime parity.
CLI tests define user-facing command and setup behavior and must stay parity-reviewed.
TUI package and documentation deltas can affect distributed interactive UX behavior and must be reviewed against Hermes Ultra's Rust-primary UX surface.
TUI source differences affect interactive UX/runtime surfaces.
Agent tests cover model routing, provider, memory, and loop semantics.
Run-agent tests cover orchestration and provider execution semantics.
TUI package differences affect distributed interactive UI behavior.
CLI test deltas cover command contracts and user-visible runtime behavior.
Autonomous-agent skill catalog content is curated locally while runtime behavior is governed by Hermes Ultra's native skill loader and execution contracts.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while Rust runtime parity is tracked through native skill loader contracts.
Disk cleanup plugin differences affect user-facing maintenance tooling and need runtime parity review before clearance.
Local disk-cleanup delta is a behavior-equivalent tuple membership form covered by focused plugin contracts; no upstream runtime feature is missing.
Achievements plugin dashboard differences affect bundled plugin UX and packaging behavior.
Video generation plugin differences affect media tool routing and provider packaging behavior.
Local xAI video generation wording intentionally refers to the SuperGrok subscription credential path while preserving shared xAI credential resolution behavior.
Memory plugin differences affect recall/provider integration behavior.
Model-provider plugin differences affect runtime provider compatibility.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while loader and execution contracts carry runtime parity.
ACP tests cover protocol compatibility and session behavior.
Google Meet plugin differences affect connector/plugin runtime behavior.
Image generation plugin differences affect media tool routing, packaging, and runtime connector behavior.
Platform plugin differences affect adapter surface parity.
Plugin tests cover loader, hook, and runtime integration behavior.
Teams pipeline plugin differences affect platform integration behavior.
Local Teams CLI delta is a behavior-equivalent tuple membership guard covered by focused plugin contracts.
Local Teams meeting error handling delta is a behavior-equivalent tuple membership guard covered by focused plugin contracts.
Local Teams artifact model delta is a behavior-equivalent tuple membership guard covered by focused plugin contracts.
Local Teams runtime config delta is a behavior-equivalent tuple membership guard covered by focused plugin contracts.
Cron tests cover scheduling/runtime behavior.
Provider tests cover model transport compatibility and error handling.
Kanban plugin differences affect plugin runtime behavior.
Observability plugin differences affect telemetry/runtime diagnostics.
Local Langfuse README intentionally documents the Hermes tools credential flow while preserving the manual enable path.
Local Langfuse manifest intentionally advertises the Hermes tools enablement path in addition to manual plugin enablement.
Website top-level package, sidebar, localization, and static API files support documentation publishing and are not Rust runtime behavior unless covered by more specific functional classifications.
Website build/generation scripts differ for local documentation publishing and do not affect Hermes runtime.
Example dashboard plugin differences affect plugin packaging and UI integration samples.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while runtime skill contracts remain tracked separately.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while runtime skill contracts remain tracked separately.
Honcho plugin tests cover memory/plugin behavior.
Skill tests cover loader, guard, and execution semantics.
Stress tests cover runtime reliability envelopes.
Website source differs for documentation/product presentation and does not change Rust runtime behavior.
Repository metadata affects text normalization and generated artifact handling, not runtime behavior.
The upstream Python uv lock check is retained but adapted to skip cleanly when this Rust-first repo has no root pyproject.toml or uv.lock, while still failing partial uv project files.
CI workflow differences affect release and parity gate enforcement.
